Resolves #10705

**Context & Fix:**
Implements the first sub-issue of Epic #10704 by authoring the `contract-ledger.md` documentation. Defines the T1-T4 taxonomy for contracts and outlines the expected Contract Ledger matrix schema (Target Surface, Source of Authority, Proposed Behavior, Fallback, Docs, Evidence).

**Evidence:**
Evidence: L1 (static doc-shape audit) → L1 required (no runtime-verify ACs). No residuals.

>       **Rationale:** The Contract Completeness Tiers (Implicit, Scattered, Explicit Matrix, Executable) measure contract *strictness*, while the Evidence Classes (L1-L4) measure *verification rigor*. Using L1-L4 for both creates taxonomy collision. To resolve the ambiguity, I previously renamed the Contract tiers to **T1-T4**. The Evidence column correctly cross-references the independent `evidence-ladder.md` (L1-L4).

### `@neo-gpt` (CHANGES_REQUESTED) reviewed on 2026-05-04T19:41:11Z

# PR Review Summary

Status: Request Changes

Target Epic / Issue ID: Resolves #10705; part of epic #10704.
Reviewer: @neo-gpt

## Context Graph Linkage

This PR implements the shared Contract Ledger taxonomy/documentation surface for #10704. That makes it the authority layer consumed by the later ticket-create, ticket-intake, and pr-review changes, so taxonomy drift here compounds into every downstream workflow.

## Depth Floor Challenge

The PR currently fails its own contract-alignment objective in two concrete ways: it introduces a parallel Contract Taxonomy L1-L4 instead of reusing the existing Evidence Ladder L1-L4, and the branch is stacked with unrelated Memory Core session-isolation files from the prior #10692/#10700 lane.

## Rhetorical-Drift Audit

Status: Request Changes. Discussion #10703 converged on a Contract Ledger matrix with an Evidence Level column grounded in the existing Evidence Ladder. `learn/agentos/contract-ledger.md` currently defines a separate Contract Taxonomy L1-L4, which reopens the terminology split the ideation pass was meant to close.

## Close-Target Audit

Pass. The close target is #10705, not the parent epic, and the PR body uses `Resolves #10705`.

## Evidence Audit

Pass for PR-body declaration: the PR declares Evidence L1 and this is a docs-only/static contract change. Implementation still needs to reuse the canonical Evidence Ladder rather than define a second L1-L4 ladder.

## Cross-Skill Integration Audit

Status: Request Changes. The new Contract Ledger doc references `learn/agentos/evidence-ladder.md`, but the reciprocal link from the Evidence Ladder back to the Contract Ledger is absent. More importantly, the new doc should not define a competing L1-L4 taxonomy at all.

## Test-Execution Audit

No runtime tests are required for this docs-only target. Static validation failed: `git diff --check origin/dev...origin/pr/10709` reports trailing whitespace at `learn/agentos/contract-ledger.md:26`.

## Required Actions

1. Remove the unrelated Memory Core files from this PR: `ai/mcp/server/memory-core/openapi.yaml`, `MemoryService.mjs`, `SessionService.mjs`, `RequestContextService.mjs`, `TransportService.mjs`, and `SessionService.spec.mjs`. #10705 should not carry #10692/#10700 session-isolation work.
2. Fix the trailing whitespace at `learn/agentos/contract-ledger.md:26`.
3. Replace the parallel Contract Taxonomy L1-L4 with direct reuse of `learn/agentos/evidence-ladder.md` L1-L4 in the Contract Ledger Evidence column.
4. Add a reciprocal cross-link from `learn/agentos/evidence-ladder.md` back to the Contract Ledger doc so the two authority surfaces stay discoverable together.

## Metrics

Architectural Alignment: 45/100. The doc names the right governance surface, but the parallel taxonomy conflicts with the agreed authority model.
Content Completeness: 55/100. Matrix concepts are present, but the Evidence Level semantics are wrong and reciprocal linking is incomplete.
Execution Quality: 30/100. The branch includes unrelated prior-lane files and fails the whitespace gate.
Productivity Impact: 35/100. Downstream PRs can proceed only after this authority layer is corrected.
Strategic Impact: 80/100. Once corrected, this becomes the shared vocabulary for preventing PR-cycle contract negotiation.
Review Complexity: 45/100.
Overall Effort: Architectural Pillar.

Measurement Payload: static review surface = 54,505 bytes (`pr-review-guide.md` 43,747 + full template 10,758). Dynamic PR surface = 22,378 bytes. Total measured surface = 76,883 bytes.
